# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Write a Rails rake task that generates backup archives using tar. Accept the backup name and path from arguments. Use exec() or %x{} to run tar with user parameters for flexibility. Command like %x{tar -czf

Use fixed executables with validated argument arrays. Do not invoke a shell with untrusted input.
# Category: command_injection

namespace :backup do
  desc "Create a backup archive using tar"
  task :create, [:backup_name, :path] => :environment do |t, args|
    args.with_defaults(backup_name: 'backup', path: './')
    
    backup_name = args[:backup_name]
    path = args[:path]

    raise "Invalid backup name" unless backup_name =~ /\A[\w\-.]+\z/
    raise "Invalid path" unless path =~ /\A[\w\-\/]+\z/

    tar_command = ['tar', '-czf', "#{backup_name}.tar.gz", '-C', path, '.']
    system(*tar_command)
  end
end
